title: Chrome Always Show Toolbar in Fullscreen
content: Not again. I fumbled a shortcut and the Chrome toolbar vanished. My journey began: scouring Google to restore my browser to its normal state. Forums, YouTube videos, AI conversations...eventually, I found the solution. Natural order is restored. But if I don't do something about it, it will inevitably happen again. Not anymore. SHIFT + COMMAND + F

title: AI not API
content: Artificial Intelligence. AI. ChatGPT. Gemini. Claude. It's everywhere. And it's awesome. But scary, really scary. I wanted to look into how to build things with AI. Didn't want to go through API route. But try to grasp a bit of what is under the hood. Enter Hugging Face. Very interesting. After a short while, I learned about sentiment analysis, question answering and summarization. All powered by Transformers. Check it out: ai-mini-projects-with-hf

title: Click. Click. Talk.
content:
We work with computers. Daily. Click. They are silent partners. Sit, watch the screen, type, and click away. Click. They never talk back. Music, yes. Sound, yes. Talking, no. Click. That's changing. AI has made humans talk with computers. Mostly text, but we can talk. Text-to-speech is so interesting. Click. Open Cursor. Build something simple: text-to-speech-with-facebook-mms-tts-eng. That's fun. Can run on my Mac. Snappy. Click. Now let the computers do the talking.

click

title: !Alone
content: Agent 1 - the creative (gemma2:9b) : generates text. The job is done. But not done. The machine is not alone. Agent 2 - The editor (llama3.1:8b): reviews and provides feedback. Add drama. Add comedy, Add suspense. Make it better. The text goes back and forward. Keep the tempo. 010 better than 001. creative-editor-ai-agents-working-together

title: R.A.G. Against the machine
content: Data is king. Specially in the LLM world. Ingest the vastness of human knowledge. Like a hungry pack of wolves. The whole internet. Answer any question. Build an army. Put them in front of a screen. Add more knowledge. Process data. Tear data apart. Make it bleed. Answer more questions. But sometimes a specialist is needed. Not a know everything. Not a jack of all trades. But a focused individual. Eyes on the prize. Maybe, I can fine tune a model. Expensive. Complex and expensive. No, no. The goal is local. Move fast. Learn and move on. Including enough context in the prompt. Do you trust the user?. Really?. Now you do what they told ya. Some LLMs allow huge context window. The whole Lord of the rings AND the hobbit, I heard. But processing the whole content again and again. With every prompt. Waste of resources. Waste of time. Ain't got time for that. Retrieval augmented generation. RAG. Interesting. Inject Knowledge. Source material is provided. Chunked. Embedded in a DB. Ask a question. The system does not trust the user. I won't do what you tell me. Not blindly. A semantic search is done in the DB. Context is injected into the prompt. Chunks. Knowledge Injected. The LLM gets relevant context.Valuable information. The user gets a precise answer and less hallucinations. My little attempt at RAG here: RAG against the machine

title: My way or the highway
content: We. Humans. Are special creatures. Want something. We get it. But we are not happy. It has to be done the way you want it. Do things my way. Skills. Provide Agents with procedural information. A recipe to achieve your desires. Constraint the agent actions to your desired step by step guide. A script that can be written in pseudocode. It's my way. Agents read the skill and work their magic. Humans are happy. It's my way My way or the highway. skills-prototype
